mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 250 in the CPIAUCSL index triggers central bank rate hikes, which increase discount rates and reduce equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 250 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust supply-side activity, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint growth of INDPRO and HOUST fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 60% of cases, or their correlation with SPX returns drops below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A monetary tightening cycle, indicated by rising FEDFUNDS and elevated CPIAUCSL, increases borrowing costs and reduces corporate earnings potential, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL exceed thresholds despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market, which increases wage pressures and corporate costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 130k and UNRATE falls below 10% despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) and rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust demand-side momentum, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The joint growth of GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce positive 60d returns in > 60% of cases, or their correlation with SPX returns drops below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) paired with elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals supply-side bottlenecks and inflationary pressures, which increase risk premiums and reduce S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 90 and CPIAUCSL exceeds 255 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: A late-cycle labor market, indicated by high PAYEMS and low UNRATE, signals impending monetary tightening, which reduces equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 130k and UNRATE falls below 10% despite the signal.
